images.caption: Figure 1:(a) VMME-V2 Pareto across QA model sizes.GridProbevariants in the green region Pareto-dominate the 2B baseline.(b) Compute reduction acrossKKat fixed 2B QA.

Figure 2:GridProbepipeline. Stage 1:2​K2Krow/column probes onK2K^{2}candidate frames yield an importance map. Stage 2: one focused pass on the top-MeffM_{\mathrm{eff}}cells, sized adaptively from the map’s distribution shape.

Figure 3:Encoder-space (top) vs answer-space (bottom) selection signals. Encoder-space scoring computes scalar similarity from independent vision and text encoders, while answer-space scoring reads the probe confidence directly from the QA VLM’s posterior over answer candidates.

Figure 4:GridProbe’s adaptiveMeffM_{\mathrm{eff}}(blue) and the 2B baseline accuracy (red), smoothed across signedskew​(M)\mathrm{skew}(M)on V2 (K=12K{=}12,n=3,200n{=}3{,}200). The two curves mirror each other: both signed extremes route to smallMeffM_{\mathrm{eff}}on intrinsically easier questions, while the near-uniform middle gets near-K2K^{2}coverage on intrinsically harder ones, an empirical realization of the redundancy principle (§3.3).

Figure 5:Three Video-MME-v2 queries exercise three distribution-shape regimes (K=12K{=}12). Theσ\sigmastatistic adaptsMeffM_{\mathrm{eff}}from140140(holistic) to55(specific). On the specific query,GridProbeanswers correctly with55frames while theK2=144K^{2}{=}144baseline fails. Notably, MDP3 (a powerful encoder-space selector with its paper-default fixed budget ofM=8M{=}8) misses both the holistic and specific cases.
